data.title: ispace Seals $2.5 Million Lunar Rover Payload Deal With Korea’s UEL for Mission 3
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from ispace,inc. ( (JP:9348) ).

ispace has signed a final payload service agreement with Korea’s Unmanned Exploration Laboratory to transport and support a two-wheeled lunar rover on the company’s Mission 3 to the Moon. The deal, valued at 2.5 million dollars, builds on an earlier interim agreement between the firms and underscores growing cross-border cooperation in lunar robotics, although ispace says the contract will not affect its earnings forecast for the current fiscal year.

Under the agreement, ispace will provide end-to-end payload services, including technical integration, transport via third-party rockets, operations on the lunar surface, and related data communications support. The collaboration with UEL, a privately held space robotics start-up focused on construction-capable lunar rovers, enhances ispace’s role as a service provider in lunar exploration and may strengthen its competitive position in future resource and infrastructure projects on the Moon.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:9348) stock is a Hold with a Yen608.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ispace,inc. stock, see the JP:9348 Stock Forecast page.

More about ispace,inc.

ispace, inc. is a Japan-based space exploration company focused on providing lunar transportation and payload services using its own landers and rovers. The company operates on a mission-by-mission basis, delivering customer cargo to the Moon and supporting lunar surface experiments and data communications, positioning itself within the emerging commercial lunar economy.

Average Trading Volume: 2,051,041

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: Yen82.75B

Learn more about 9348 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Kaili Resources Files Updated ASX Governance Key and Confirms Compliance Statement
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Kaili Resources Limited ( (AU:KLR) ).

Kaili Resources Limited has lodged its Appendix 4G and confirmed that its up-to-date corporate governance statement for the year ended 31 December 2025 is available on its website in line with ASX Listing Rule requirements. The board-approved statement, current as of 31 March 2026, outlines compliance with key ASX Corporate Governance Council recommendations, including maintaining a formal board charter, conducting checks on directors and senior executives, and having written agreements that define roles and responsibilities.

The disclosure demonstrates Kaili Resources’ adherence to the ASX’s governance framework by mapping where each governance practice is documented and confirming that the company secretary reports directly to the board through the chair. This filing serves both as a transparency tool for investors and a verification mechanism that the company has met mandatory governance disclosure obligations, reinforcing its compliance posture and governance discipline as an ASX-listed entity.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:KLR) stock is a Hold with a A$0.17 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kaili Resources Limited stock, see the AU:KLR Stock Forecast page.

More about Kaili Resources Limited

Kaili Resources Limited is an Australia-listed company that operates under the regulatory framework of the ASX and is subject to the ASX Corporate Governance Council’s principles and recommendations. As a listed entity, it is required to maintain and disclose robust governance structures, including a board charter, appointment practices, and accountability arrangements for directors and senior executives.

data.title: Kali Metals shareholders back capital moves at general meeting
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kali Metals Limited ( (AU:KM1) ) has provided an update.

Kali Metals Limited has reported the results of its 31 March 2026 general meeting, where shareholders strongly backed all resolutions, including the ratification of earlier placement share issues under Listing Rules 7.1 and 7.1A. The approvals also covered the issue of director placement shares to board members, the granting of broker options, and the ratification of an agreement to issue consideration shares to SQM, reinforcing the company’s capital management plans and support for its ongoing exploration and growth initiatives.

The resounding votes in favour of these measures indicate solid shareholder confidence in Kali Metals’ funding strategy and governance, providing the company with flexibility to incentivise key participants and advance its portfolio of gold, lithium and critical mineral projects. These outcomes are likely to strengthen Kali Metals’ position in the Australian exploration sector as it seeks to progress and expand its asset base across several highly prospective regions.

More about Kali Metals Limited

Kali Metals Limited is an Australian mineral exploration company focused on gold, lithium and other critical minerals across 4,029 square kilometres of tenure in Western Australia’s Pilbara and Eastern Yilgarn regions, as well as the Southern Lachlan Fold Belt in New South Wales and Victoria. The company’s strategy centres on exploring, developing commercial resources and adding new strategic gold, lithium and tin assets to drive shareholder value.

An update from Noble Helium Ltd. ( (AU:NHE) ) is now available.

Noble Helium has requested and been granted a trading halt on its securities by the ASX, with the pause to remain in place until no later than the start of trading on 2 April 2026. The company said the halt is linked to an upcoming announcement addressing an ASX price and volume query and a proposed capital raising.

The move signals that Noble Helium is preparing to disclose material information that could affect its valuation and liquidity, including details of new funding plans. Investors will be watching for clarity on the capital structure and any explanations for recent trading activity, which may influence sentiment toward the company’s growth strategy and risk profile.

data.title: Infotrust Completes $50m Divestment to Sharpen Cyber Security Focus
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Spirit Technology Solutions Ltd. ( (AU:ITS) ) just unveiled an update.

Infotrust Ltd has completed the divestment of its Cloud & Communications segment, including 100% of Nexgen Investment Group, to Aussie Broadband for total consideration of up to $50 million. The deal delivers $44.1 million in upfront cash and up to $5.9 million in contingent payments tied to Nexgen’s EBITDA performance in FY26 or FY27, with Infotrust to provide transitional services for a limited period.

The transaction marks a significant milestone in Infotrust’s strategic shift toward becoming a focused cyber-first technology services provider and materially strengthens its balance sheet. The proceeds are expected to support the company’s cyber security growth strategy and acquisition pipeline, potentially enhancing its competitive position in Australia’s cyber security and managed technology market.

More about Spirit Technology Solutions Ltd.

Infotrust Ltd is a leading Australian sovereign cyber security and managed technology provider, offering integrated services and resilient technology solutions that help organisations remain secure, compliant and connected. The company focuses on cyber-first technology services, targeting clients that require robust security, regulatory compliance and reliable technology infrastructure across Australia.

EMC Gold Corporation has published its updated corporate governance statement for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, confirming alignment with the ASX Corporate Governance Council’s principles as at 30 March 2026. The company has formalised a detailed board charter clarifying the respective roles of directors and management, including responsibilities for strategic direction, executive appointments, risk management, major capital decisions and the integrity of financial reporting and market disclosure, reinforcing governance transparency for shareholders.

The board retains approval over key matters such as appointing and overseeing senior executives, setting strategic objectives, reviewing internal controls and compliance, and supervising external audit processes. Pengana Global Private Credit Trust ( (AU:PCX) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Pengana Global Private Credit Trust has updated the market on changes to its capital structure, confirming the cessation of 514,917 ordinary fully paid units on the ASX under the ticker PCX. The units were cancelled on March 30, 2026 as part of an on-market buy-back, reflecting the trust’s active management of its issued capital base.

The buy-back driven cancellation reduces the total number of units on issue, which can be supportive of net asset value per unit for remaining investors and may indicate confidence in the underlying portfolio. This adjustment to capital structure may also influence the trust’s trading liquidity and is a routine step in aligning its market float with capital management objectives.

data.title: Podium Minerals’ New Chairman Backs Parks Reef as Strategic PGM Asset
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Podium Minerals Ltd. ( (AU:POD) ) has provided an announcement.

Podium Minerals’ new chairman Neal Froneman used the March shareholder meeting to outline his reasons for joining the board and his belief in the strategic value of the Parks Reef PGM project. He highlighted the project’s compelling orebody, its Australian location in a low‑risk jurisdiction and the recent build‑out of an experienced leadership team positioned to execute development plans.

Froneman pointed to recent metallurgical test work on the high‑grade hanging wall zone, which delivered higher product grades and improved PGE and copper recoveries versus bulk sulphide feed, underscoring the project’s technical and economic potential. He added that funds raised in November have left the balance sheet well placed to advance drilling, flowsheet optimisation and development studies, reinforcing Podium’s ambition to become a strategic supplier of critical PGMs to global markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:POD) stock is a Sell with a A$0.04 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Podium Minerals Ltd. stock, see the AU:POD Stock Forecast page.

More about Podium Minerals Ltd.

Podium Minerals Ltd. is an Australian mining company focused on developing the Parks Reef platinum group metals project in Western Australia. The company aims to become a domestic PGM producer, leveraging a technically advanced concentrator flowsheet and a strengthened leadership team with deep experience in PGMs, processing and project development.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Brookfield Business Corp. Class A ( (TSE:BBUC) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Brookfield Business Corporation has filed its 2025 annual report on Form 20‑F, including audited financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025, with U.S. and Canadian securities regulators, and made the documents available online and in hard copy to shareholders. The company also completed a corporate simplification in which all outstanding partnership and exchangeable units were converted into newly issued class A subordinate voting shares of Brookfield Business Corporation on a one‑for‑one basis, with these shares set to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange and Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbol BBUC, consolidating its public market presence and potentially enhancing clarity and accessibility for investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:BBUC) stock is a Hold with a C$50.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Brookfield Business Corp. Class A stock, see the TSE:BBUC Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on BBUC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BBUC is a Neutral.

The overall stock score is primarily impacted by significant financial challenges, including declining revenue, high leverage, and negative equity. Technical analysis provides some positive momentum, but valuation remains a concern with negative earnings and low dividend yield. The absence of earnings call data and corporate events limits further insights.

To see Spark’s full report on BBUC stock, click here.

More about Brookfield Business Corp. Class A

Brookfield Business Corporation is a global owner and operator of vital industrial and business services operations, focused on acquiring market‑leading companies, executing operational improvement plans to boost cash flows, and recycling capital to drive long‑term growth. It serves as the flagship vehicle of Brookfield Asset Management’s Private Equity Group, benefiting from the scale and resources of a leading global alternative asset manager with over $1 trillion in assets under management.
